message:
test(security): add regression tests for security fixes in 1fcd949

Add 22 targeted tests across 5 crates ensuring security fixes have
explicit coverage and cannot silently regress:

fcp-sandbox/macos.rs (10 tests):
- SBPL path sanitization rejects injection via quotes, parens,
  backslashes, and newlines
- Profile generation with malicious readonly/writable paths produces
  sanitized output without injected SBPL directives

fcp-sandbox/egress.rs (6 tests):
- Constant-time SPKI pin comparison matches first/last pin, rejects
  mismatched content and length, handles empty pin list, catches
  single-byte differences

fcp-oauth/token.rs (4 tests):
- Debug impl redacts access_token, refresh_token, and id_token
- Non-sensitive fields (token_type, scopes, issued_at) remain visible

fcp-protocol/session.rs (2 tests):
- current_timestamp returns nonzero on normal systems
- Pre-epoch clock simulation produces 0 instead of panicking

fcp-raptorq/decode.rs + codec/decoder.rs (5 tests):
- k saturates at u32::MAX for huge transfer lengths
- Zero symbol_size handled via max(1)
- checked_mul overflow returns recoverable InsufficientSymbols error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f4sw","title":"[IDEA-25] RaptorQ Parameter Auto-Tuning + Benchmarks","description":"## Background
Symbol size and repair parameters strongly impact performance and MTU safety. Current defaults are static and may be suboptimal per platform/path.

## Goals
- Add auto-tuning for symbol size and repair symbol count.
- Provide benchmark suite to compare configurations.
- Emit recommended parameters for common MTUs (LAN/DERP).

## Non-goals
- Changing the canonical wire format; only tune parameters.

## Approach
- Add a bench harness in `fcp-raptorq` and `fcp-cli bench` integration.
- Record decode/encode time, memory, and overhead.
- Store recommended presets in config.

## Risks
- Overfitting to benchmarks; keep conservative defaults and allow override.

## Success Criteria
- Bench suite produces consistent metrics across runs.
- Recommended parameters reduce p99 latency without MTU regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Bench suite outputs p50/p95/p99 and memory usage JSON.
- Preset table covers LAN and DERP MTU defaults.

[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use prefix sums for availability queries.
- Priority queue for repair task scheduling.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f50","title":"fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting","description":"# fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable controlled Reddit participation:
- submit posts
- submit comments
- edit/delete own content

These are real-world side effects and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Submit post to a subreddit (text/link).
- Submit comment.
- Edit content.
- Delete content.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.post.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.comment.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.content.edit` (dangerous)

Policy guidance:
- Posting should often require ApprovalToken depending on zone policy.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Content safety:
  - outbound content must be treated as a “publication” action
  - receipts + audit events include subreddit + post/comment id
  - never log post bodies

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.post.submit`
  - `reddit.comment.submit`
  - `reddit.content.edit`
  - `reddit.content.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - request formation
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - error mapping (subreddit rules, forbidden)

## Acceptance criteria
- Write operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

- fcp-sandbox/macos.rs: 10 tests for SBPL path sanitization (injection via quotes, parens, backslashes, newlines) + 2 profile generation with malicious paths
- fcp-sandbox/egress.rs: 6 tests for constant-time SPKI pin comparison (match first/last, no match, length mismatch, single byte diff, empty pins)
- fcp-oauth/token.rs: 4 tests for Debug redaction (access_token, refresh_token, id_token not leaked, non-sensitive fields preserved)

All 928 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T07:40:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fgot","title":"[IDEA-01.1] SDK runtime kit: trait surface + config schema","description":"## Goal
Define the minimal trait boundary and config schema for the runtime kit so streaming/polling supervisors can be used by connectors with platform-specific state.

## Details
- Add `SupervisorConfig` (backoff, jitter, retry budget, cooldown, shutdown timeout, max_consecutive_failures).
- Define connector-facing trait hooks:
  - `StreamingSession` (resume token, sequence, heartbeat state)
  - `PollingCursor` (offset/seq + persistence hooks)
- Define explicit health transition rules (Healthy → Degraded → Unhealthy) and expose `HealthSnapshot` updates.

## Tests
- Unit tests for config defaults and validation (reject nonsensical values).
- Compile-only tests in `fcp-sdk` to ensure trait ergonomics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Traits are stable enough to be used by both Discord- and Telegram-like connectors.
- Config defaults align with study docs (1s base, 60s cap, jitter on).","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

Ready to close when blocker flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 is closed.","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:16:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.29","title":"fcp.annas-archive: Anna's Archive Book & Document Search Connector","description":"# fcp.annas-archive - Anna's Archive Book & Document Search Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Anna's Archive connector for search/retrieval under strict NetworkConstraints and explicit policy/legal constraints.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 76/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (Research/Knowledge family).

**Why Critical**: Anna's Archive is the **largest open book search engine**, aggregating data from multiple library sources including Open Library, Library Genesis, Z-Library, and more. Enables agents to discover and access books, textbooks, and documents for research, learning, and knowledge work.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (Research + Knowledge Access)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 3 Enterprise (weeks 9-12)

### Related Connectors
- **ArXiv**: Academic preprints (papers)
- **Semantic Scholar**: Citation-enhanced paper search
- **Anna's Archive**: Books and documents
- Together: comprehensive knowledge access

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge]
```
- **Knowledge**: Provides knowledge/search capabilities (read-only pattern)

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: No mesh-persisted state needed
- Each search is independent
- Results are not cached in connector state

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: External API connector
- Memory isolation for consistent behavior
- Cross-platform search capability

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    host_allow: vec\![
        "annas-archive.org".into(),
        "annas-archive.se".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
Search results contain external metadata:
```rust
// Search results are externally sourced
search_result.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// If results contain download URLs:
if result.contains_urls {
    search_result.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `annas.search` | Safe | Read-only search |
| `annas.metadata` | Safe | Read-only metadata |
| `annas.lookup.isbn` | Safe | Read-only lookup |
| `annas.lookup.md5` | Safe | Read-only lookup |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Search & Discovery
- Full-text book search
- Author and title search
- ISBN/DOI lookup
- File format filtering
- Language filtering

### Metadata Access
- Book metadata (title, author, year, publisher)
- File information (format, size)
- Source information
- Cover images

### Knowledge Workflows
- Textbook discovery
- Reference material lookup
- Reading list compilation
- Document research

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Find textbooks on machine learning"
"What books has this author written?"
"Look up this book by ISBN"
"Find technical documentation for React"
"Search for books about system design"
"Get metadata for this title"
"Find books published in 2023 about AI"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### ArXiv/Semantic Scholar Integration
**Complete knowledge stack:**
- ArXiv for cutting-edge papers
- Semantic Scholar for citations
- Anna's Archive for foundational books
- Unified research capability

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- Index book metadata
- Semantic search over literature
- Build knowledge base

### Vector DB Integration
- Book embeddings for discovery
- Similar book recommendations
- Topic clustering

### AI Provider Integration
- Summarize book descriptions
- Generate reading recommendations
- Knowledge extraction

### Agent Mail Integration
- Book recommendations → agent threads
- Reading list collaboration
- Research material sharing

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
Anna's Archive provides:
- **Search API**: Full-text and metadata search
- **Metadata API**: Book/document details
- **File Info**: Format and availability

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `scraper` — HTML parsing if needed

### Identifier Support
Multiple ID formats:
- MD5 hash (primary identifier)
- ISBN-10 and ISBN-13
- DOI
- OpenLibrary ID
- Custom IDs per source

### Response Data
```json
{
  "md5": "abc123...",
  "title": "Book Title",
  "author": "Author Name",
  "publisher": "Publisher",
  "year": "2023",
  "language": "en",
  "extension": "pdf",
  "filesize": 15000000,
  "coverurl": "https://..."
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:knowledge:annas:full` | Full access | Search and metadata |
| `z:knowledge:annas:search` | Search only | Discovery |
| `z:knowledge:annas:metadata` | Metadata only | Book details |

### Data Considerations
- Metadata is informational
- Links to external sources
- Respect source terms of service

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Knowledge/search provider

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `annas-archive.org:443`
- `annas-archive.se:443` (alternate domain)

### Credential Injection
- No authentication typically required
- Rate limiting is IP-based
- API keys if available via `CredentialId`

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `annas.search` — Search books/documents
- `annas.metadata` — Get book details
- `annas.lookup.isbn` — ISBN lookup
- `annas.lookup.md5` — Direct lookup

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Search Books
```
GET /search?q=machine+learning&lang=en&ext=pdf
```

Response:
```json
{
  "results": [
    {
      "md5": "abc123...",
      "title": "Hands-On Machine Learning",
      "author": "Aurelien Geron",
      "year": "2022",
      "extension": "pdf",
      "filesize": 50000000,
      "language": "en"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Get Metadata by MD5
```
GET /md5/{md5}
```

### ISBN Lookup
```
GET /isbn/{isbn}
```

### Search Parameters
| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `q` | Search query |
| `lang` | Language filter (en, es, de, etc.) |
| `ext` | File extension (pdf, epub, mobi) |
| `year_from` | Minimum year |
| `year_to` | Maximum year |
| `sort` | Sort order |

---

## Knowledge Workflow Patterns

### Textbook Discovery
1. Search by topic/course
2. Filter by recency
3. Get metadata and reviews
4. Compile reading list
5. Track reading progress

### Reference Lookup
1. User mentions a book
2. Search by title/author
3. Return metadata
4. Provide availability info

### Research Support
1. Receive research topic
2. Search for foundational texts
3. Cross-reference with papers
4. Build comprehensive bibliography

---

## Rate Limiting

### Best Practices
- Reasonable request rate
- Cache search results
- Respect robots.txt
- Conservative approach

### Implementation
```rust
// Rate limiter: conservative
let limiter = RateLimiter::new(10, Duration::from_secs(60));
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Search query construction
- Response parsing
- ISBN validation
- Metadata extraction

### Integration Tests
- Mock API responses
- Multi-result scenarios
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Rate limit enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real API calls in unit tests
- Search queries functional
- Metadata retrieval works
- ISBN/MD5 lookup works
- Multiple formats supported
- Language filtering works

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3","title":"fcp.sentry - Sentry Error Tracking Connector","description":"# fcp.sentry - Sentry Error Tracking Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Deliver the FCP2 Sentry connector epic plan for error/alert ingestion and workflows with strict NetworkConstraints and safe event handling.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 85/100** — High priority Tier 2 connector.

**Why Critical**: Sentry is the **leading error tracking platform** for developers. Real-time error monitoring is essential for autonomous debugging workflows. Enables agents to investigate errors, correlate issues, and close the loop on software quality.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Developer Productivity)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Error Investigation
- Query error events and issues
- Access stack traces and context
- View error frequency and trends
- Get affected user counts

### Issue Management
- Triage issues (assign, resolve, ignore)
- Link issues to releases
- Track regression status
- Manage issue status

### Release Tracking
- Associate errors with deploys
- Track release health
- Monitor crash-free rates
- Compare release performance

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"What's causing the spike in 500 errors?"
"Show me the stack trace for this issue"
"Mark this issue as resolved in version 2.1.0"
"Find all unhandled exceptions in the last hour"
"Which release introduced this regression?"
"Create a Jira ticket for this Sentry issue"
"Correlate this error with Datadog metrics"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### GitHub Integration
**Close the debugging loop:**
- Sentry issue → GitHub issue/PR
- Stack traces → code navigation
- Suspect commits identification

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Auto-create issues from Sentry alerts
- Link error tracking to project management
- Track fix status bidirectionally

### Datadog Integration
**Full observability correlation:**
- Correlate errors with metrics
- Link traces to error events
- Infrastructure context for debugging

### Agent Mail Integration
- Error alerts → agent investigation threads
- Coordinate debugging across team
- Escalation workflows for critical errors

### Browser Connector Synergy
- Reproduce reported errors
- Capture state at error time
- Automated error verification

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Issues API** — Query and manage issues
- **Events API** — Access error event details
- **Projects API** — Project configuration
- **Releases API** — Release tracking
- **Webhooks** — Real-time error alerts

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
- Auth Token (org-scoped or user-scoped)
- DSN for sending events (not needed for this connector)
- Rate limits vary by plan

### Data Model
```
Organization
└── Project
    └── Issue (grouped errors)
        └── Event (individual occurrence)
            ├── Exception (stack trace)
            ├── Breadcrumbs (context)
            └── Tags/Context
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:sentry:full` | Full access | All read/write operations |
| `z:ops:sentry:readonly` | Read-only | Query issues and events |
| `z:ops:sentry:project:<slug>` | Project-scoped | Single project access |
| `z:ops:sentry:triage` | Triage only | Update issue status |

### Data Sensitivity
- Error context may contain user data
- Stack traces reveal code structure
- Breadcrumbs may contain sensitive actions

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST CRUD operations
- `streaming` — Webhook event ingestion

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `sentry.io:443` (SaaS)
- Self-hosted instance endpoints as configured

### Credential Injection
- Auth token via `CredentialId`
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `sentry.issues.read` — Query issues
- `sentry.events.read` — View event details
- `sentry.projects.read` — Project info
- `sentry.releases.read` — Release data

### Write Operations
- `sentry.issues.update` — Update issue status
- `sentry.issues.assign` — Assign issues
- `sentry.issues.resolve` — Mark resolved
- `sentry.releases.create` — Create releases

### Administrative (Dangerous)
- `sentry.issues.delete` — Delete issues
- `sentry.projects.admin` — Project settings

---

## Operations (Representative)

### List Issues
```json
GET /api/0/projects/{org_slug}/{project_slug}/issues/
Query params:
  query: is:unresolved level:error
  statsPeriod: 24h
  sort: freq
```

### Get Issue Details
```json
GET /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/
```

Response includes:
```json
{
  "id": "123",
  "title": "TypeError: Cannot read property 'x' of undefined",
  "culprit": "app/components/Widget.tsx in render",
  "count": "1234",
  "userCount": 89,
  "firstSeen": "2024-01-10T...",
  "lastSeen": "2024-01-15T..."
}
```

### Get Latest Event
```json
GET /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/events/latest/
```

Response includes stack trace:
```json
{
  "exception": {
    "values": [{
      "type": "TypeError",
      "value": "Cannot read property 'x' of undefined",
      "stacktrace": {
        "frames": [...]
      }
    }]
  },
  "breadcrumbs": {...}
}
```

### Update Issue Status
```json
PUT /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/
{
  "status": "resolved",
  "statusDetails": {
    "inRelease": "2.1.0"
  }
}
```

---

## Autonomous Debugging Workflow

### Error Investigation Pipeline
1. Sentry webhook triggers on new/spike
2. Agent queries issue details and stack trace
3. Agent correlates with recent deploys
4. Agent searches codebase for affected code
5. Agent proposes fix or creates issue ticket
6. Agent monitors for regression after deploy

### Integration Pattern
```
Sentry Alert
    ↓
Agent Mail Thread Created
    ↓
Agent investigates:
  - Stack trace analysis
  - Recent commit correlation
  - Datadog metric correlation
    ↓
Either:
  - Auto-fix (simple cases)
  - Create Linear/Jira issue
  - Escalate to human
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Issue query construction
- Event parsing (stack traces)
- Status update formatting
- Release association

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based Sentry API simulation
- Multi-event correlation scenarios
- Webhook payload processing

### E2E Compliance
- Validates default deny + NetworkConstraints
- Project-scoped access verified
- Audit logging for all operations

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Sentry calls in unit tests
- Issue query and listing functional
- Event/stack trace access works
- Issue status updates work
- Release tracking operational
- Webhook events processed

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","title":"fcp.sentry: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.sentry: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Sentry connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** for strict FCP2 compliance.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (events/issues/performance data reads)
  - `operational` (issue assignment/status updates, alert rules)
  - `streaming` (webhooks: issue events, alert triggers, releases)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin Sentry API host (SaaS: sentry.io; self-hosted: configured base host)
  - pin attachment/artifact download hosts if distinct
  - HTTPS-only; deny redirects by default
- State model declaration:
  - webhook cursor/idempotency state if persisted
  - mapping state for auto-create-beads feature (if stored)

## Output artifacts
- Full manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad examples for static compliance.

## Acceptance criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.
- Static compliance can mechanically reject overly-broad egress.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:19:36.227777549Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-02T02:21:07.709845Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T02:21:07.709816Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":423,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Created connectors/sentry/manifest.toml with 17 operations across 4 capability families (sentry.read, sentry.write, sentry.alerts, sentry.admin). Covers all 3 archetypes (knowledge/operational/streaming). Operations: list_issues, get_issue, list_issue_events, get_event, discover_query, get_transaction, list_projects, list_releases, get_release, list_release_deploys, update_issue, delete_issue, list_alert_rules, create_alert_rule, update_alert_rule, delete_alert_rule, stream_webhook_events. Network pinned to sentry.io + *.sentry.io + $sentry_host. State model: singleton_writer with webhook cursor/idempotency/mapping state. Validated: fcp manifest fix --check passes. Good/bad test vectors created.","created_at":"2026-03-02T02:21:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.2","title":"fcp.sentry: Provisioning Automation (auth, projects, webhooks)","description":"# fcp.sentry: Provisioning Automation (auth, projects, webhooks)

## Goal
Automate Sentry onboarding (auth + project binding + webhook setup) with minimal human steps.

## Requirements
- Auth via `CredentialId` (token) for either:
  - Sentry SaaS, or
  - self-hosted base URL (policy-owned)
- Project/environment selection:
  - validate project slug/org
  - validate environment filters (prod/staging)
- Webhook setup automation:
  - register webhook(s) for issue/alert/release events
  - validate webhook signature/HMAC (if supported)

## Safety rules
- Validate credentials with safe read-only calls.
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning before readiness.
- Never log tokens or DSNs.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Mockable in CI.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.3","title":"TEST: Sentry Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Sentry Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)

## Goal
Comprehensive mock-only tests for fcp.sentry (issue/event/perf reads, webhooks, and safe write ops).

## Requirements
- No real Sentry API calls in CI.
- Mock endpoints for:
  - issue search/list
  - event detail fetch (stack traces)
  - performance transactions
  - release associations
  - alert rule config
  - webhook event ingestion
- Tests for:
  - tainting of webhook inputs
  - capability gating for reads vs writes
  - dangerous ops (rule changes) require approval
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic CI suite.
- Logs include correlation_id + reason codes for deny paths.
